ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class (FOODON_03315091). No specific prawn-and-pork filled dumpling subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Dumplings, filled pasta and similar products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers filled pasta and dumplings as a category. No specific prawn-pork dumpling code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for prawn and pork dumplings. SNOMED CT does not provide granular food product codes at this level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 8 handmade dumplings, approximately 160 g total, as sold by Din Tai Fung in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Dumplings, pork and shrimp (boiled) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Chinese-style boiled dumplings
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Din Tai Fung product entry exists in approved databases. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for boiled pork-and-shrimp dumplings (jiaozi) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA data for Chinese boiled dumplings. Per-100g values reflect a typical handmade prawn-and-pork dumpling with thin wheat-flour skin. Serving size of 160 g (8 pieces × ~20 g each) is estimated from standard dumpling weight references.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — boiled dumplings / filled pasta analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Din Tai Fung prawn-and-pork dumplings. GI of 48 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled wheat-flour dumplings / filled pasta (GI range 39–55, mean ~48). The thin wheat-flour skin and protein-rich filling moderate the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 28.0) / 100 = 13.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~608 mg per 8-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 4–6 pieces and avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce, as a single 8-piece serving contributes approximately 25–30% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate content with low GI (GI ~48); glycemic load ~13.4 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI of the wheat-flour dumpling skin combined with the protein and fat from prawn and pork filling moderates postprandial glucose rise; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to one 8-piece serving per meal and pair with non-starchy vegetables to manage total glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~1.6 g per 100 g) and sodium (~380 mg per 100 g) from pork filling and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Prawn and pork dumplings contain moderate saturated fat and sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume no more than one 8-piece serving and avoid additional sodium from dipping sauces to keep total saturated fat and sodium within heart-healthy dietary targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Handmade Prawn and Pork Dumplings (水饺, shuǐjiǎo) are a classic Chinese boiled dumpling filled with a mixture of fresh prawns (shrimp) and minced pork, seasoned with ginger, sesame oil, soy sauce, and spring onion, enclosed in a thin, hand-rolled wheat-flour skin. This frozen product is sold in portions of 8 pieces and is associated with Din Tai Fung, a globally recognised Taiwanese-origin restaurant chain with a prominent presence in Singapore. The dumplings are cooked by boiling from frozen until the skin becomes tender and slightly translucent, yielding a juicy, savoury filling with a delicate sweetness from the prawn. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 148 kcal, 9.2 g protein, 4.8 g fat, and 17.5 g carbohydrate. The low glycemic index (~48) reflects the moderating effect of the protein-fat filling on carbohydrate digestion. Sodium content is notable (~380 mg per 100 g) due to seasoning, making portion control relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The prawn component contributes lean protein, iodine, and vitamin B12. These dumplings are not halal due to the pork content and contain gluten (wheat) and crustacean allergens. They are a popular dim sum and home-meal item across Singapore and the broader Chinese diaspora.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Din Tai Fung prawn and pork dumplings?
Are prawn and pork dumplings healthy?
What is the GI of Chinese dumplings?
What allergens are in prawn pork dumplings?

confidence_reason: No exact product-level nutrient data exists for Din Tai Fung's handmade prawn and pork dumplings in any approved primary database. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for boiled pork-and-shrimp dumplings and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat-flour dumplings. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA source data for this food category. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in handmade boiled dumplings per USDA data. Serving size of 160 g (8 pieces) is estimated at approximately 20 g per piece, consistent with standard dumpling weight references. This product contains pork and is therefore not halal. Allergens include wheat gluten, crustaceans (prawn), soy, and sesame. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct measurement for this specific product was found in approved GI sources. Glycemic load per serving = (48 × 28.0 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 13.4.

Added Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names to satisfy Singaporean-cuisine G6 requirement.
Populated aliases_by_language for all five mandatory Singaporean languages.
Verified all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes.
Documented trans_fat_g = 0.0 as confirmed trace/negligible in notes.
Set glycemic_index to 48 (numeric > 0) with analogous estimate basis from University of Sydney GI Database.
Used only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none' for all ontology match_type fields.
Included specific clinical guidance for all three health conditions including Type 2 Diabetes.
